prompt:
I inherited the bike-share dock rebalancing branch after our contractor wrapped up, and it will not get past the type-check. CI runs `bash run_verify.sh` (npm ci first — node_modules isn't committed), and tsc says:

  rebalance.ts(18,10): error TS18048: 'st.lastReport.bikes' is possibly 'undefined'.
  rebalance.ts(25,67): error TS2339: Property 'Standby' does not exist on type '{ readonly Active: "active"; readonly Reserve: "reserve"; readonly Offline: "offline"; }'.
  rebalance.ts(35,28): error TS2339: Property 'deficit' does not exist on type 'T'.
  report.ts(4,21): error TS7006: Parameter 's' implicitly has an 'any' type.
  report.ts(6,20): error TS7006: Parameter 'n' implicitly has an 'any' type.
  report.ts(14,19): error TS2339: Property 'sort' does not exist on type 'readonly Station[]'.
  stations.ts(28,38): error TS2749: 'DockState' refers to a value, but is being used as a type here. Did you mean 'typeof DockState'?

The planning rules themselves are considered settled — the half-rack targets, which docks get skipped, the wall-board ordering guarantees — and test_bikedock.ts pins all of it, so where the code is ambiguous the tests are the spec. Please get the verify green with focused fixes: no loosening the compiler options, no restructuring modules, and leave test_bikedock.ts, tsconfig.json, run_verify.sh and the two package manifests exactly as they are.
notes: Seven independent planted compile errors, one diagnostic each, all surfacing in a single `tsc -p tsconfig.json` pass with typescript 5.9.3 (pinned devDependency, Part C deps mechanics: npm ci via reference_setup): optional-chain null-safety in a comparison (overCapacity), renamed const-object member (DockState.Standby vs Reserve), generic missing its `extends` constraint (totalShortfall), two implicit-any callback params (report.ts helpers), readonly-array mutation (.sort in rankStations — tests also pin that the input snapshot is not reordered, so deleting `readonly` fails the suite), and a const object used in type position without a companion type alias (TS2749). node --test is the second gate. Toolchain observed at authoring: node 26.3.0, npm 11.16.0, tsc 5.9.3.
